top_issues.issue: Module-level mutable arrays (serverStack, requestStack) shared across all test files — global mutable state breaks test isolation
opts._retryCount written onto the caller's options object as a hidden mutation sentinel to prevent auto-refresh retry loops
onRefresh hook uses Node.js error-first callback convention (err, cb) while the entire library is Promise-based — mixed async paradigms
_apiAuthRequest has a hidden side-effect: it writes to this.oauth in single-user mode, while transport methods should not manage credential state
getAttachmentBody, getDocumentBody, and getContentVersionData are structurally identical — six-line duplication across three functions
Repeated opts.resource / opts.method / return this._apiRequest(opts) pattern in six consecutive GET methods
_rehandshake and _scheduleReconnect both contain identical subscription re-subscription loops
opts parameter bag has no defined shape — 20+ properties with no JSDoc typedef, making the API contract implicit
WebSocket frame constants (0x80, 0x7f, 0x0f, 126, 127, 0x81, 0x88) appear as unexplained magic numbers without named constants
_connectLoop handles six distinct responsibilities: sending connect, parsing responses, detecting failure codes, dispatching data messages, applying intervals, and error recovery
